detaileddescription.para:
Enable SSIT Single EAM Event forwarding from Slave SLRs to Master SLR for this specific error interrupt
XPLMI_SSIT_SINGLE_EAM_EVENT_ERR_ID is the Error NodeId
XPLMI_SSIT_SINGLE_EAM_EVENT_ERR_MASK is the Error Event Mask
XPLMI_SSIT_SINGLE_EAM_EVENT_ERR_TRIG is the trigger register address
On Master SLR, this interrupt will trigger the same error in the local EAM

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the local SLR index.
This function is used to get the combined mask of all slave SLRs.

simplesect.para: Returns combined mask of all slave SLRs

briefdescription.para: This function is used to register any event between Master and Slave SLRs.

parameterdescription.para: is the Index of the event to be registered
is the handler to be executed when this Event occurs
is the SLR origin which can trigger the event

briefdescription.para: This function is used to write the event buffer data and trigger the Message Event.

parameterdescription.para: is the index of the SLR to which the event buffer need to be written
is the buffer from where the event buffer data to be written to Slave SLRs event buffer
is the size of the ReqBuf

briefdescription.para: This function is used to read the event buffer of Master SLR in Slave SLRs where the message event is received.

parameterdescription.para: is the buffer to which the event buffer data to be written
is the size of the ReqBuf

briefdescription.para: This function is used to trigger the given event to the given SLR.

parameterdescription.para: is the index of the SLR to which the event to be triggered
is the Index of the event to be triggered

briefdescription.para: This function is used to wait for the event to be acknowledged by the given SLR after triggering.

parameterdescription.para: is the SLR index where the event is triggered
is the Index of the event triggered
is the timeout in us to wait for the acknowledgment

briefdescription.para: This function is used to read the response (if applicable) in Master SLR once the event is acknowledged by Slave SLR.

parameterdescription.para: is the SLR index from the response need to be read
is the Response Buffer to where the response to be written
is the size of the response buffer

briefdescription.para: This function is used to write the response (if applicable) in Slave SLR once the event is handled and acknowledge the message event.

parameterdescription.para: is the Response Buffer data to be written
is the size of the response buffer

briefdescription.para: This function is used to acknowledge the event after handling.

parameterdescription.para: is index of the SLR for which the event was triggered
is the event to acknowledge

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the global SLR address for the given local address.

parameterdescription.para: is the local address for which the global SLR address need to be calculated
is the index of the SLR for which the address need to be calculated

memberdef.argsstring: (u8 SlrIndex, u32 *ReqBuf, u32 ReqBufSize, u32 *RespBuf, u32 RespBufSize, u32 WaitForEventCompletion)
memberdef.name: XPlmi_SsitSendMsgEventAndGetResp

briefdescription.para: This function sends a message event with given request buffer to a slave SLR, waits for event completion, reads the response from the slave SLR and returns it to the caller.

parameterdescription.para: is the SLR index from which the response need to be read
is the buffer to which the event buffer data to be written
is the size of the ReqBuf
is the Response Buffer to where the response to be written
is the size of the response buffer
is the maximum time to wait before the requested message event is handled by a slave SLR [in microseconds]
holds the inputs of the slaves
Stores the response of the slaves

listitem.para: XPLMI_SSIT_INTR_NOT_ENABLED if interrupts are not enabled.

briefdescription.para: This function provides SSIT config secure communication command execution.

para.verbatim: Command payload parameters are * SLR number, base address of buffer which consists of IV1,IV2,Key in master * IV1(128-bit), IV2(128-bit) and Key(256-bit) in slave

parameterdescription.para: is pointer to the command structure

briefdescription.para: This function gives SSIT secure communication status Command payload parameters are.

para.verbatim: * SLR index for which secure communication status is requested

parameterdescription.para: is pointer to the command structure

listitem.para: Returns the Status of get secure communication status command

briefdescription.para: This function provides SSIT Sync Master command execution.

parameterdescription.para: is pointer to the command structure

simplesect.para: Returns the Status of SSIT Sync Master command

listitem.para: Initiate synchronization to PLM running on master SLR
Wait for Master SLR to reach synchronization point
Clear existing status to know the actual status from Master SLR

briefdescription.para: This function provides SSIT Sync Slaves command execution.

para.verbatim: Command payload parameters are * Slaves Mask * Timeout

parameterdescription.para: is pointer to the command structure

listitem.para: Returns the Status of SSIT Sync Slaves command
XPLMI_ERR_SSIT_SLAVE_SYNC if master timedout on slave sync.
Wait until all Slaves initiate synchronization point.
Clear existing status to know the actual status from Slave SLRs.
If the timeout is exceeded, return an error code.

briefdescription.para: This function provides SSIT Wait Slaves command execution.

para.verbatim: Command payload parameters are * Slaves Mask * Timeout

parameterdescription.para: is pointer to the command structure

listitem.para: Returns the Status of SSIT Wait Slaves command
XPLMI_ERR_SSIT_SLAVE_SYNC if master timedout on slave sync.
Clear any existing SSIT errors in PMC_ERR1_STATUS register.
Wait until all Slaves initiate synchronization point.
If the timeout is exceeded, return an error code.
